name: RAG Agent
description: Retrieval-augmented generation system for document Q&A, knowledge base search, or internal tool assistance. Ingests and chunks documents, embeds with text-embedding-3-small, stores in ChromaDB, and retrieves relevant context for Sonnet to generate grounded answers.

defaultStack.rationale: Zero infra, survives restarts via persistent storage, sufficient for <500k chunks. Upgrade path: Qdrant Cloud for multi-user or remote access.
text-embedding-3-small: $0.02/MTok, strong retrieval quality. Switching cost is low — embeddings are swappable if ChromaDB collection is rebuilt.
Strong instruction-following for grounded answers with citations. Use system prompt to enforce 'only answer from retrieved context' constraint.
Balances context density with retrieval precision. Overlap prevents answer truncation at chunk boundaries.
Stateful graph handles retrieval retry, reranking step, and answer generation with tool use. Simpler than custom chain wiring.
Cover the three most common enterprise document types. Pluggable parser interface for adding new formats.
Exposes /query endpoint for UI or downstream consumers. Pydantic validates request shape and enforces max_tokens on the client side.

phaseHints: Phase 1 — Ingestion pipeline: document parsers per format, recursive chunking, text-embedding-3-small batch embedding, ChromaDB collection with metadata schema
Phase 2 — Retrieval & reranking: similarity search top-k, optional Haiku reranker to score chunk relevance, retrieval quality eval on test queries
Phase 3 — Generation & grounding: Sonnet with retrieved context in system prompt, citation extraction, 'I don't know' fallback when retrieved score < threshold
Phase 4 — API & eval: FastAPI /query endpoint, test set of 50 Q&A pairs, answer faithfulness scoring via Sonnet-as-judge, latency benchmarking

architectureDecisions.choice: Hybrid: dense vector search + BM25 keyword search, results merged with RRF
architectureDecisions.rationale: Dense retrieval misses exact keyword matches (model names, version numbers). BM25 covers lexical gaps. RRF fusion avoids score normalization complexity.

architectureDecisions.choice: System prompt constraint + post-generation faithfulness check via Sonnet-as-judge on 10% of queries
architectureDecisions.rationale: Prompt constraint is first line. Faithfulness check catches hallucinations that slip through. 10% sample balances cost vs. coverage.

architectureDecisions.choice: source_file, page_number, section_title, ingested_at, chunk_index stored alongside vector
architectureDecisions.rationale: Enables citation rendering (page N of Document X) and filtered retrieval by source or date without re-embedding.

costModelHint: Ingestion: text-embedding-3-small at $0.02/MTok — 1000 pages ≈ 500k tokens ≈ $0.01 one-time. Per query: Sonnet input ~3k tokens (context + question) + ~500 output = ~$0.017/query. At 100 queries/day: ~$1.70/day. Haiku reranker adds ~$0.002/query if enabled.
